commit.message:
feat(baseline): add baseline comparison and anomaly detection system

Implements "what's different from usual" detection for watch points:

- src/baseline/baseline_store.py: Store baselines and deviations with SQLite
  - BaselineMetric: Statistical baseline (mean, std_dev, min, max)
  - Deviation: Detected anomaly with z-score and severity
  - Sample management for baseline calculation

- src/baseline/metrics_extractor.py: Extract metrics from reports
  - Text metrics: word/sentence/paragraph counts
  - Entity counts: PERSON, ORG, DATE, MONEY, PERCENT
  - Sentiment indicators: positive/negative/uncertainty/urgency
  - Keyword frequencies and topic detection

- src/baseline/deviation_detector.py: Unified anomaly detection
  - Quantitative deviations (z-score based)
  - Sentiment shifts
  - Topic drift (keyword disappearance)
  - Silence detection (term disappearance)
  - New entity emergence

- src/routers/baseline.py: API endpoints
  - POST /api/baseline/establish - create baseline from history
  - POST /api/baseline/compare - detect deviations
  - GET /api/baseline/{watch_id} - get baseline metrics
  - GET /api/baseline/deviations/{watch_id} - list deviations

- tests/test_baseline.py: 28 tests covering all functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace HTTP polling signaling with WebSocket (#119)

* feat: replace HTTP polling signaling with WebSocket server

Replace the Val Town HTTP polling signaling server with a Go WebSocket
server backed by SQLite, deployed on fly.io. Connection setup drops from
~15s (3 round-trips × 5s polling) to under 1s (instant WebSocket push).

- Add signaling-server/ (Go + gorilla/websocket + modernc.org/sqlite)
- Rewrite wail-net signaling client to use tokio-tungstenite WebSocket
- Rewrite test server to axum WebSocket with fair round-robin polling
- Remove poll_interval_ms from all connect APIs
- Update SIGNALING_URL to wss://wail-signal.fly.dev
- Delete val-town/main.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review issues in WebSocket signaling server

- Fix double-join panic: join() now leaves previous room before
  registering in a new one, preventing stale *conn references that
  would cause send-on-closed-channel panics
- Fix signal from-field spoofing: server now uses authenticated
  c.peerID instead of client-provided msg.From
- Fix /rooms info disclosure: filter out password-protected rooms
- Add warn logging for sendJSON drops and signal relay drops
- Delete stale Val Town deploy workflow
- Update DEVELOPMENT.md, docs/networking.md, tradeoffs.md to reflect
  WebSocket signaling architecture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix load-previous duplicates: handle WSY products appearing on multiple sheets

Products on "What's HOT" duplicate products from other sheets with the same
article code. key_to_idx previously mapped to the LAST index, causing both
the manual order and loaded-previous to appear as separate entries.

Now maps key → ALL indices, skips loading if any row already ordered,
and uses the first index (original sheet) to avoid duplicates.

commit.message:
fix(admin): add .vercel/output to turbo build outputs

Nitro with the vercel preset generates .vercel/output/ (Build Output
API) but turbo.json only cached .output/** and .tanstack/**. On cache
hit turbo replayed logs without restoring the .vercel/output/ directory,
causing Vercel to fall back to framework detection and fail with
"No Output Directory named dist found".

commit.message:
fix: POST + URLSearchParams via proxy for EU Search API

EU API requires POST; FormData (multipart) was unreliable through proxy.
Switch to URLSearchParams (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) which proxies cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix build_Wr_B Hartree permutation bug; clarify Case C comment

build_Wr_B: W^{cdab}(-r) = conj(W^{dcba}(r)) for Case B hermiticity
[W^{abcd}(τ)]* = W^{dcba}(τ), not W^{badc}(τ) as previously assumed.
Correct permutation is (4,3,2,1), not (2,1,4,3). Using (2,1,4,3) would
have given W^{cdab}(+r) instead of W^{cdab}(-r), wrong for complex W.

build_Wr_C: rewrite comment to list all four W(-r)=conj(W^{hgfe}(r))
mappings explicitly; code was already numerically correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

variants: "no tengo acceso directo para ver", "no tengo la capacidad

no false positives on normal responses.

- GoogleAnalytics / PageViewTracker コンポーネントを新規作成し layout.tsx に組み込み

  push_unsubscribe, ical_copy, family_invite, share_target_upload

  (GitHub Secrets: GA_MEASUREMENT_ID を各 environment に設定)
- NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID 未設定時は自動無効 → E2E テストへの影響なし

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use window-capture tracked pointer type in FilteredPencilBrush

Previous issue: FilteredPencilBrush read ev.e.pointerType but Fabric.js
may pass a synthesized MouseEvent (no pointerType property), causing the
type check to be skipped and all input types to draw unchecked.

Fix:
- Move pointer type tracking from upperCanvas to window with {capture:true}
  so the ORIGINAL PointerEvent is captured before any browser/Fabric
  event synthesis
- FilteredPencilBrush.onMouseDown reads lastDrawTypeRef (set by window
  capture) instead of ev.e, making it immune to Fabric's event conversion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

2. Add cambio_duracion to _P1_SKIP_TYPES (prevents unsolicited photos after duration/pricing responses)
3. Skip persuasive text generation when response indicates no-availability
4. Don't clear existing dates in session when availability instructions rebuild
5. Only transition to QUOTED state when reserva_precio_total > 0 (no-availability responses stay in BROWSING)
6. Fix _fechas_timestamp → reserva_fechas_timestamp naming mismatch (dates always appeared stale)

commit.message:
feat(reload): add in-progress guard, POST /v1/reload endpoint, and mm-cli reload command

- mailStore.triggerReload(force?) coalesces concurrent reload requests: if a
  reload is in progress the next call is queued as a single follow-up run so
  no reload is silently dropped. Returns 'triggered' or 'queued'.
- Auto-reload callbacks in mailStore.init now go through triggerReload so the
  guard applies to file-watcher-triggered reloads too.
- ReloadAPI: POST /v1/reload (authenticated) triggers a force-reload and
  returns { reload: 'triggered' | 'queued' }.
- TemplateClient.triggerReload() calls the new endpoint.
- mm-cli reload command triggers a server-side force-reload via the API.

fix: path correcto para archivos estáticos del frontend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: D2A peer discovery — relax thresholds, extend expiry, add diagnostics

Root cause of "0 Peers": three compounding issues prevented peer discovery
even when both users had linked Nostr accounts and enabled D2A.

1. Interest threshold mismatch: broadcastPresence used ≥0.2 for interests
   but calculateResonance required ≥0.3 — topics broadcast by peers could
   never match the stricter discovery filter. Unified to 0.2 via shared
   INTEREST_BROADCAST_THRESHOLD constant.

2. PEER_EXPIRY_MS too short (15 min): peers had to be online simultaneously
   within a 15-minute window. Extended to 1 hour so presence events survive
   across sessions.

3. RESONANCE_THRESHOLD too strict (0.3 Jaccard): even a single shared
   interest out of 4 total gave Jaccard=0.25, below threshold. Relaxed
   to 0.15 so one overlapping topic is sufficient for discovery.

Also added diagnostic logging:
- broadcastMyPresence logs interest count and top topics
- discoverAndNegotiate logs topic count when no peers found
- discoverPeers logs pre/post resonance filter with per-peer details

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[Agent] feat(backup): manual Backup & Restore (#1005) (#2662)

* feat(backup): add manual Backup & Restore feature (#1005)

- Add BackupManager in PVLibrary with async backup/restore API
  - Supports selective backup of: Realm DB, save states, custom
    artwork, and battery saves
  - Creates .pvbackup zip archives via SSZipArchive
  - Restores by extracting archive and replacing target directories
  - Realm DB restore staged as default.restored.realm; takes effect
    on next app launch
- Add BackupRestoreView SwiftUI settings screen
  - Per-component toggles (database / saves / artwork / battery)
  - Progress reporting during backup and restore operations
  - Share sheet (iOS) or Documents folder save (tvOS) for exports
  - File picker import on iOS; Documents-scan restore on tvOS
- Expose PVMediaCache.cachePath as public for BackupManager access
- Wire BackupRestoreView into LibrarySection2 in SettingsSwiftUI
  for both iOS and tvOS settings

Part of #1005

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address AI review — backup imports, DB restore, data safety

- Add missing imports (PVFileSystem, PVMediaCache, RealmSwift) to
  BackupManager.swift that prevented compilation
- Use Realm.writeCopy(toFile:) for consistent DB snapshot during backup,
  replacing unsafe fm.copyItem that could capture mid-write state
- Add applyPendingRealmRestoreIfNeeded() to RomDatabase.initDefaultDatabase()
  so default.restored.realm staged by BackupManager is swapped in on launch
- Fix restoreDirectory() to copy to temp location first then atomically swap,
  eliminating data loss if copy fails after old destination is deleted
- Fix duplicate Backup & Restore entry on tvOS (wrap non-tvOS link in
  #if !os(tvOS) guard)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multi-provider LLM abstraction layer (#1)

* feat: add multi-provider LLM support (Ollama, Groq, OpenRouter)

Adds server/src/services/llm.ts as a provider abstraction so the app
can run without an Anthropic API key during development. Supported
providers:

  LLM_PROVIDER=ollama      # local, completely free (default model: llama3.2)
  LLM_PROVIDER=groq        # free tier, set GROQ_API_KEY
  LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter  # free models available, set OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic   # original paid path (default)

Also refactors claude.ts to use the abstraction and removes the
non-standard output_config JSON schema parameter (not a real SDK
feature). Recipe JSON is now extracted via prompt instruction + Zod
validation, which works across all providers. Startup logs now show
the active provider and give actionable hints when a key is missing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_011wZtovmndXMX4zTzntMena

* chore: add server/package-lock.json

Tracks exact dependency versions for the server after running npm install.

https://claude.ai/code/session_011wZtovmndXMX4zTzntMena
